title: alopecurus pratensis subsp. pratensis
intro: Alopecurus pratensis subsp. pratensis, also known as meadow foxtail or green foxtail, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of meadow foxtail include its ability to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of meadow foxtail include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the dried plant in hot water or a solvent, and are often used to treat a range of health issues.
